title: JIT IR translation — binary matching and construction
purpose: Binary pattern matching (bs_match, bs_get_integer, bs_get_binary, bs_get_utf8) and construction (bs_init, bs_put_integer, bs_put_binary) are performance-critical for protocol handling and data parsing. JIT-compiling these to native code with runtime helper calls avoids interpreter dispatch overhead for each segment extraction or append. Binary operations are complex (match context state, heap allocation for sub-binaries) and use runtime helpers rather than inline IR for the actual memory operations.
task: Implement IR translation for binary matching and construction opcodes in crates/beamr/src/jit/ir_binary.rs. Matching opcodes compile to runtime helper calls that advance a match context. Construction opcodes compile to runtime helper calls that build binary segments on the process heap. All heap-allocating operations emit GC safepoints via B-134's SafepointEmitter.

requirements.title: Binary match context runtime helpers
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL provide extern 'C' runtime helpers for binary matching: jit_bs_start_match(process, binary) -> match_context, jit_bs_get_integer(match_ctx, size, flags) -> term, jit_bs_get_binary(match_ctx, size) -> term, jit_bs_test_tail(match_ctx, expected_bits) -> bool. Each helper operates on a MatchContext that tracks the current bit position. THE SYSTEM SHALL emit a GC safepoint before jit_bs_get_binary (allocates a sub-binary on heap).
acceptance: jit_bs_start_match creates a MatchContext from a binary Term
jit_bs_get_integer extracts an integer of given size and advances position
jit_bs_get_binary extracts a sub-binary and advances position (heap allocation + safepoint)
jit_bs_test_tail checks remaining bits equal expected
RuntimeHelpers struct extended with binary matching function pointers
Test: match <<1:8, 2:16>> -> extracts 1 and 2 correctly

requirements.title: Binary matching IR translation
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL translate bs_match, bs_get_integer, bs_get_binary, bs_get_utf8, bs_test_tail, and bs_test_unit opcodes into Cranelift IR that calls the corresponding runtime helpers. WHEN a binary match fails (pattern mismatch), THE SYSTEM SHALL branch to the fail label. THE SYSTEM SHALL track the match context as a live root in the SafepointEmitter.
acceptance: translate_bs_match(builder, emitter, binary, fail_label) emits jit_bs_start_match call + fail check
translate_bs_get_integer(builder, emitter, match_ctx, size, flags, dest, fail_label) emits helper call
translate_bs_get_binary(builder, emitter, match_ctx, size, dest, fail_label) emits safepoint + helper call
Match context is tracked as live root across all safepoints
Test: compiled binary match <<A:8, B:16, Rest/binary>> extracts A, B, Rest correctly
Test: compiled binary match on non-binary input branches to fail

requirements.title: Binary construction runtime helpers and IR
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL provide runtime helpers for binary construction: jit_bs_init(process, size_hint) -> builder, jit_bs_put_integer(builder, value, size, flags), jit_bs_put_binary(builder, binary), jit_bs_finish(builder) -> binary_term. THE SYSTEM SHALL translate bs_init, bs_put_integer, bs_put_binary, and bs_finish opcodes into Cranelift IR calling these helpers. THE SYSTEM SHALL emit a GC safepoint before jit_bs_init (heap allocation).
acceptance: translate_bs_init(builder, emitter, size_hint, dest) emits safepoint + jit_bs_init call
translate_bs_put_integer(builder, bs_builder, value, size, flags) emits helper call
translate_bs_put_binary(builder, bs_builder, binary) emits helper call
translate_bs_finish(builder, bs_builder, dest) emits helper call returning tagged binary Term
Test: compiled <<1:8, 256:16>> -> valid 3-byte binary
Test: compiled <<Head/binary, Tail/binary>> concatenation -> correct result

requirements.title: UTF-8 binary operations
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL translate bs_get_utf8, bs_get_utf16, bs_get_utf32, bs_put_utf8, bs_put_utf16, and bs_put_utf32 opcodes into runtime helper calls that handle Unicode encoding/decoding. IF a UTF operation encounters invalid encoding, THE SYSTEM SHALL branch to the fail label (matching) or raise a badarg error (construction).
acceptance: jit_bs_get_utf8(match_ctx) extracts one UTF-8 codepoint and advances position
jit_bs_put_utf8(builder, codepoint) encodes and appends one UTF-8 codepoint
Invalid UTF-8 during match branches to fail label
Invalid codepoint during construction raises badarg
Test: match <<"hello"/utf8>> extracts 5 codepoints
Test: construct <<0x1F600:32/utf32>> -> correct 4-byte encoding

requirements.title: Opcode dispatch wiring for binary opcodes
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL update the main opcode dispatch in compiler.rs to route bs_match, bs_get_integer, bs_get_binary, bs_get_utf8, bs_test_tail, bs_test_unit, bs_init, bs_put_integer, bs_put_binary, bs_finish, bs_get_utf16, bs_get_utf32, bs_put_utf8, bs_put_utf16, and bs_put_utf32 opcodes to the ir_binary.rs translations instead of emitting deopt exits. THE SYSTEM SHALL leave bs_append and binary comprehension opcodes as deopt.
acceptance: All listed binary opcodes route to ir_binary.rs translations in the compiler dispatch
bs_append and binary comprehension opcodes still deopt to interpreter
Test: function with binary match + construction compiles without deopt

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement bit-level binary matching inline — all binary operations go through runtime helpers
SHALL NOT optimise binary append (the append optimisation requires tracking binary references, deferred)
SHALL NOT implement binary comprehensions — deoptimise to interpreter
verification: cargo test -p beamr passes with all tests green
ir_binary.rs is under 500 lines of production code
All heap-allocating binary operations have GC safepoints
